author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Combined Order Convention 1985 - Questions and Answers
content:
SANGHARAKSHITA IN SEMINAR
QUESTION AND ANSWER SESSIONS
COMBINED ("MIXED") ORDER CONVENTION 1985
CONTENTS



1 The general inadequacies of the question and ans wer sessions
2 Chanting in the FWBO Not injecting emotion int o mantra chanting
The magical-cum-scientific and the devotional appro ach to chanting
3 Bhante's general avoidance of the use of the lan guage of chakras and currents
etc. Using Buddhist calendar dates rather than Christian ones
4 Tension between the world and those trying to le ad a spiritual life
5 Using Bhante's seminar tapes and using teachings that particularly appeal -
the Precious Garland seminar
6 Buddhist iconography and symbolism The figure o f the Buddha only being
originally portrayed by symbols
7 The need to re-evaluate everything that has come down through the Buddhist
tradition to see what is essential
8 Not using images in Muslim countries Traditional attachment to robes and objects of wors hip
Symbols. The precious metals of the Ten Pillars
Bhante's views on alternative medicine Dependence on the contact with the
practitioner
8-9 The need to question one's motives if practisi ng medicine
9-10 Donation of organs after death - does it affe ct the consciousness? An act of
dana A need to reassess our whole attitude towards life and death
11-13 Next Session Being in debt and reasons for this - Not taking responsibility
for oneself Being infantile lack of imagination
Not just talking about individuality but having to practise it!
14 Becoming self-supporting through Right Liveliho od businesses as opposed to
signing on the dole - creating a strong financial b asis for all activities
15 Buddhism's role in the educational system?
16 Order members becoming involved with other non- Centre but Buddhist
related activities such as meditation for world pea ce
17 "Where have all the Bodhisattvas gone?" The eleven headed, thousand armed Avalokiteshvara a s a symbol of the Order
18 The disaster of triviality! Training oneself to lead study
Using a dictionary Having access to dictionaries o f one's own language and
Pali and Sanskrit
19 Bhante's attitude to computers The possible da nger of alienation in computer
programmers
20 Talking about the FWBO in its entirety through the Newsletter
21 The difficulty of expressing things in the writ ten word
Joy in new books being written by Order members and not just Bhante.
